commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=d1f2715fcbce1fdd02f601a0a0f5dd3ac2e1b825)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a managed npm-root downgrade guard in `src/plugins/install.ts` and unit tests for the new predicate.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection gives a high-confidence path: preserving skips the dependency rewrite, the lockfile remains on the newer version, and existing verification still compares it against the older incoming `npmResolution`.
commands.review_followup: The PR needs an author or maintainer repair plus better real behavior proof; ClawSweeper should not auto-repair an external PR while the proof gate still requires contributor action.

target.title: fix(plugins): prevent openclaw update from silently downgrading managed deps
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #85184. Prevents `openclaw update` from silently downgrading a newer already-installed managed direct dependency.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: `openclaw update` re-synced externalized bundled-plugin direct dependencies through installPluginFromManagedNpmRoot, which unconditionally overwrote the managed npm root dependency. A user with a newer pinned/installed version (a deliberately applied hotfix) had it silently downgraded. This adds a narrow guard that keeps the newer installed version when an update would downgrade it.

Real environment tested: Linux (Ubuntu 24.04, WSL2), Node.js 24.14.0, current PR head. Invoked shouldPreserveManagedDependencyAgainstDowngrade directly via tsx against the real code.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: ran the guard predicate against the real runtime via tsx.

Evidence after fix: stdout from the tsx invocation against the real runtime:

```
$ npx tsx proof.ts
update, installed 0.11.2, incoming 0.10.0 -> true   (downgrade; keep the newer installed version)
update, installed 0.11.2, incoming 0.12.0 -> false  (upgrade; allow)
update, installed 0.11.2, incoming 0.11.2 -> false  (same version; allow)
install mode, installed 0.11.2, incoming 0.10.0 -> false  (guard only applies on update)
update, no installed version, incoming 0.10.0 -> false  (fresh install; allow)
update, installed latest, incoming 0.10.0 -> false  (unparseable; preserve original behavior)
update, installed 0.11.2, incoming 0.11.1 -> true   (patch-level downgrade also caught)
```

Observed result after fix: For the reported shape (installed 0.11.2, update wants 0.10.0) the guard returns true, so the managed npm root write is skipped and the newer version is kept with a warning, instead of the prior silent downgrade. Normal upgrades, same-version reinstalls, install mode, fresh installs, and unparseable/range specs all return false and keep the original behavior.

What was not tested: A live mutating `openclaw update --tag` against a real user install was not run; the guard is exercised through the pure predicate under the real runtime plus focused unit tests, matching the source-level reproduction in the issue.
target.author: alkor2000
